title: System Response to Internal Conditions: The Zero-Input Response
summary: This page explains that the zero-input response of an LTIC system is a linear combination of exponential characteristic modes associated with the roots of the characteristic equation Q(λ)=0. It then introduces repeated roots and shows that when a root is repeated, the homogeneous solution changes form, for example yielding (c₁ + c₂t)e^{λt} for (D − λ)²y₀(t)=0.
